title: [Hooks] Feature: exponential backoff in babysitter-stop-hook.sh per-effectId during long agent waits
summary: Add per-effectId exponential backoff to the shared stop-hook path so repeated blocks while the same agent effect is pending produce fewer redundant assistant turns without delaying progress after the effect resolves.
implementationFocus: Implement the feature in the SDK shared stop-hook runtime rather than only in plugins/babysitter-unified/hooks/stop.sh, because that wrapper delegates to babysitter hook:run.
Identify the current pending effectId when run state is waiting and apply backoff per runId plus effectId.
Use default delay semantics matching the issue: 10 seconds, 30 seconds, 90 seconds, then cap at 300 seconds.
Expose BABYSITTER_HOOK_BACKOFF_BASE and BABYSITTER_HOOK_BACKOFF_CAP with defensive parsing.
Record effectId and actual applied delay on STOP_HOOK_INVOKED.
Ensure effect resolution during a backoff period causes prompt continuation instead of waiting for the full old delay.
acceptanceFocus: Per-effectId fire counter or equivalent journal-derived count is tracked.
Delay grows for repeated stop-hook fires on the same effectId and caps at the configured maximum.
Counter or derived state resets when EFFECT_RESOLVED or EFFECT_CANCELLED is observed for that effectId.
Run-level tracking is cleaned up or harmless after RUN_COMPLETED and RUN_FAILED.
When an effect resolves during backoff, the next hook fire happens promptly and #158-style post-resolution progress is not regressed.
BABYSITTER_HOOK_BACKOFF_BASE defaults to 10 seconds and BABYSITTER_HOOK_BACKOFF_CAP defaults to 300 seconds.
STOP_HOOK_INVOKED includes the actual applied delay and effectId.
Short tasks and breakpoint-only waits keep existing behavior.
qualityGates: Regression tests fail before implementation and pass after implementation.
Targeted hookRun tests cover default growth, cap, env overrides, reset on resolve/cancel, terminal cleanup, journal metadata, and interruptible wait behavior.
Targeted hookRun tests do not sleep for real backoff durations.
No regression for issue #158 post-resolution progress.
npm exec --yes --package=vitest -- vitest run --config vitest.config.ts packages/sdk/src/cli/commands/__tests__/hookRun.test.ts passes.
npm run test:sdk passes or any unrelated pre-existing failures are documented with evidence.
Reviewer approves compatibility with shared hook behavior and plugin wrapper expectations.
nonGoals: Do not fix issue #158 except to prevent regressions in its behavior.
Do not change broad orchestration lifecycle semantics outside stop-hook continuation/backoff handling.
Do not convert this repository process to shell subtasks.
Do not add noisy user-facing stop-hook text solely to describe backoff internals.
Do not introduce real long sleeps into automated tests.
notes: .a5c/process-library/ was not present while authoring; matching methodologies and specializations were researched under the repository process library root at library/.
The implementation should verify whether journal-derived tracking or a run-local state file best satisfies responsiveness, cleanup, and race-safety. The design phase must decide before code changes.
If a run has multiple pending effects, the trace/design phase must define deterministic current-effect selection before implementation.
